questions.text: When you're learning something new — whether it's a framework, a skill, or a random topic — how do you actually go about it? Do you dive into documentation, watch video courses, read books, build a side project, or prefer learning from people who know more than you?

Do you consider yourself someone who generally seeks out novelty and new experiences — trying new restaurants, exploring unfamiliar neighborhoods, picking up random hobbies — or do you tend to stick with what you know and love?

When you encounter a problem in your daily life — something breaks, you face a logistical challenge, or something goes wrong with your setup — how do you typically approach solving it? Are you someone who figures it out yourself through trial and error, immediately Google/YouTube it, ask someone for help, or does it depend on the problem?

What's your relationship with vulnerability — both in close relationships and more broadly? Are you someone who opens up easily, do you need time to build trust before being vulnerable, or do you prefer keeping things more guarded?
If you could have dinner with any historical figure — scientist, artist, philosopher, leader — who would you pick and what would you want to ask them?
